channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Tuesday, June 9 (game #828)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Tuesday, June 9 (game #1597)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Tuesday, June 9 (game #1094)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: Quote of the day by Mark Zuckerberg: "Move fast and break things. Unless you are breaking stuff, you are not moving fast enough" — on the nature of disruption
item.description: The Facebook co-founder has long run his company with a philosophy that promotes speed in innovation

item.title: Meta quietly removes face-recognition code from its smart glasses app
item.description: The 'disappearing into the bushes like Homer Simpson' strategy is a bold choice.

item.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
item.description: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identify verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.

item.title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
item.description: The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled. One subtle example: the many AI demos of someone standing, phone in hand.

item.title: 17 things we learned at WWDC 2026 — Siri's getting a big AI makeover, Golden Gate is the next macOS, Liquid Glass is changing, and more
item.description: Apple's WWDC 2026 keynote was short and fairly sweet. Here are the highlights.

item.title: Sam Bankman-Fried formally applies for a pardon
item.description: The FTX CEO was sentenced for fraud in 2024.

item.title: Researchers trained an open source AI search agent, Harness-1, that outperforms GPT-5.4 on recalling relevant information
item.description: A joint research collaboration between researchers at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (UIUC), UC Berkeley, and the open source AI-native vector database platform Chroma unveiled Harness-1, a 20-billion parameter open-source search agent built atop OpenAI's gpt-oss-20B open source model that fundamentally redesigns how AI executes complex retrieval tasks. Harness-1 achieves a massive leap in performance, scoring 73% average on its ability to recall relevant information correctly from a curated dataset, outperforming even GPT-5.4 (70.9%) and the next, most accurate open source sea...

item.title: Apple is using AI to fix Safari’s extension problem
item.description: Apple is trying to solve one of Safari's biggest weaknesses with AI. Safari has long lacked the robust library of extensions that its rivals have, mainly due to the stringent development requirements from Apple. But now, Apple is inviting users to essentially vibe-code their own extensions. In a demo shared by Apple, the company showed […]

item.title: AMD CEO Dr Lisa Su explains how tech will “solve some of the world’s most important problems" - but warns “we are still so early in the process”
item.description: Lisa Su says AMD is working on improving compute, says "compute actually equates to intelligence".

item.title: Where was tvOS 27 at WWDC?
item.description: Every year, Apple's Worldwide Developers Conference gives us a first look at what's coming next to the company's many operating systems. But missing from today's keynote, apart from a single graphic listing all current Apple OSes next to a big "27," was any mention of tvOS. The whole structure of this year's WWDC was different, […]

item.title: OpenAI files SEC paperwork to go public
item.description: "We expect it to leak so we're just announcing it."

item.title: Apple’s new parental controls are for keeping Apple out of trouble
item.description: When Apple put child safety front and center at WWDC on Monday, its stated goal was helping parents fine-tune their kids' online experiences and avoid excessive screen time. But amid a global debate over internet regulation, its latest updates also looked like a defensive move in a brewing fight against Meta and other app developers. […]

item.title: ‘Regulation by litigation is often overlooked as a regulatory tool’: Just how much responsibility should AI companies have on their users’ wellbeing?
item.description: AI chatbots have been likened to social media, leading to addiction and potentially worse. I explore how they could be regulated.

item.title: OpenAI files for IPO, following Anthropic
item.description: OpenAI on Monday checked off a preliminary step in the IPO race that it and rival Anthropic have been competing in for the better part of a year: The company announced it has confidentially submitted a Form S-1 with the US Securities and Exchange Commission, following Anthropic's decision to do the same on June 1st. […]

item.title: iOS 27 : voici toutes les nouveautés pour iPhone, de Siri AI aux photos retouchées par IA
item.description: iOS 27 ne mise pas sur une fonction choc, mais sur une avalanche de nouveautés tournées autour de trois idées : un Siri reconstruit, de l'IA dans presque toutes les apps, et un système qui va enfin plus vite. On fait le tour complet.

item.title: OpenAI Confidentially Files for IPO on the Heels of SpaceX and Anthropic
item.description: The ChatGPT-maker announced it has filed paperwork to go public, just a week after rival Anthropic took the same step.

item.title: Siri AI et iOS 27 : le récap complet de la WWDC 2026 d’Apple
item.description: Apple a présenté le nouveau Siri AI pendant le keynote de la WWDC 2026. Voici le récapitulatif de toutes les annonces très axées autour d'Apple Intelligence.

item.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
item.description: The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.

item.title: Apple abandonne une montre de 2023 alors que cet iPhone de 2019 survit
item.description: iOS 27 maintient une compatibilité jusqu'à des modèles sortis il y a sept ans, mais la mise à jour des Apple Watch abandonne violemment un modèle commercialisé en 2023.

item.title: Polymarket and Kalshi Say Influencer Partners Can’t Deny Election Results, Actually
item.description: Social media posts questioning the integrity of LA’s mayoral election were labeled “paid partnerships.” Then Kalshi and Polymarket told creators to delete them.

item.title: Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
item.description: Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri, signaling that the company wants users to see AI as just one part of a broader effort to improve its software.

item.title: Google cuts the price of its AI Plus plan and doubles the storage
item.description: The AI subscription now starts at $5 per month.
